question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 22, 1AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Musetti vs. Sonego: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lorenzo Musetti and Lorenzo Sonego in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 21 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.

question: Counter-Strike: B8 Academy vs Dark Tigers (BO3) - Exort Series Contenders Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between B8 Academy and Dark Tigers in the Exort Series Contenders Stage, scheduled for January 20 at 8:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "B8 Academy" if B8 Academy win the match against Dark Tigers.

This market will resolve to "Dark Tigers" if Dark Tigers win the match against B8 Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Spread: RCD Mallorca (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for February 2 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "RCD Mallorca" if RCD Mallorca win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Sevilla FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Australian Open Women's: Peyton Stearns vs Petra Marcinko
description:

This market will resolve to 'Marcinko' if Petra Marcinko advances against Peyton Stearns.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Set 1 Winner: Hijikata vs Vacherot
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Rinky Hijikata and Valentin Vacherot in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 22 2026.
This market will resolve to “Hijikata” if Rinky Hijikata wins the first set. It will resolve to “Vacherot” if Valentin Vacherot wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.

Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.

This market refers to the tennis match between Jessica Pegula and McCartney Kessler in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 22 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.

events.title: Australian Open Women's: Jessica Pegula vs McCartney Kessler
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Jessica Pegula and McCartney Kessler in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 21 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Pegula' if Jessica Pegula advances against McCartney Kessler.

This market will resolve to 'Kessler' if McCartney Kessler advances against Jessica Pegula.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
